title: triglochin hexagona
intro: Triglochin hexagona, also known as the star-dragon or six-angled meadow-saffron, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and treating skin conditions like eczema. Triglochin hexagona can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it has also been used in traditional Maori medicine for various purposes.
